name: Hallushift++ (probe baseline)
long_description: This is the baseline by the challenge provider, following the implmentation described in the Hallushift++ paper (https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.07687). The image, prompt, answer (with hallucination) pair is processed with a VLM (the suggested 7B LlaVa model) and the internal states are captured. The internal state derived features ('Hidden_layer_norms', 'max_prob', 'perplexity', 'target_prob', 'token_nll', 'token_text_len', 'token_index', 'response_len') are given to traditional ML models and MLP to predict hallucination and class types (seperate) using supervised finetuning. Random Forest was found to be best. NOTE: some features described in the paper where unable to be fetched in my run, including attention means and attention entropies. Room for improvement, different VLM and better predictor, more samples.
